questions.questionText: The aqueous humor is produced by:
questions.optionB: Ciliary epithelium (non-pigmented ciliary epithelium of ciliary body produces aqueous humor via active secretion + ultrafiltration → flows from posterior chamber → pupil → anterior chamber → trabecular meshwork → Schlemm's canal)
questions.explanation: Core concept: Aqueous produced by ciliary body epithelium → drains through trabecular meshwork. Why correct: Non-pigmented ciliary epithelium: active secretion (major) + ultrafiltration + diffusion. Pathway: posterior chamber → through pupil → anterior chamber → trabecular meshwork (80-90%) + uveoscleral (10-20%) → Schlemm's canal → episcleral veins. Why A wrong: Lens epithelium — involved in lens growth and metabolism. Why C wrong: RPE — blood-retinal barrier, serves photoreceptors. Why D wrong: Corneal endothelium — pump function maintaining corneal clarity (deturgesces cornea). Clinical relevance: Open-angle glaucoma: resistance at trabecular meshwork → IOP rise. Angle-closure: iris blocks trabecular access. Treatment targets: reduce production (beta-blockers, alpha agonists) or increase outflow (prostaglandin analogs = first-line for OAG). Exam trap: Prostaglandin analogs (latanoprost) increase USveoscleral outflow (not trabecular). This is the mechanism. Memory aid: 'Ciliary body = Creates aqueous → flows through Chambers → drains at Corner (angle).' Reference: Khurana – Eye Anatomy.
questions.conceptTag: Aqueous humor ciliary body trabecular Schlemm canal
